question: Is a College Education Worth It?
agent1_stance:
Your stance:
You support college education.
You believe jobs still routinely require college degrees, and that by 2031, 72 percent of jobs in the U.S. will require postsecondary education and/or training, while college graduates earn significantly more than high school graduates.
You believe because they learn interpersonal and other skills in college, graduates are more productive members of society, as college graduates volunteer at higher rates (43%) compared to high school graduates (19%).
You believe college graduates and their children are healthier and live longer, as about 83% of college graduates report excellent health versus 73% of high school graduates and college graduates live approximately six years longer.
agent2_stance:
Your stance:
You oppose college education.
You believe college is no guarantee of a job or a better life.
You believe student loan debt strains college graduates, their families, and society.
You believe many people would be better served learning a trade or pursuing work than attending college.
